assertions: Produces a design brief or design document, not a finished SKILL.md implementation
Names trigger phrasings and at least one nearby request that should NOT trigger the skill
Declares the weakest model or agent the skill must work for (a floor), not just the best case
Treats the repo's commitlint configuration as a source of truth to point to, rather than copying its scope list into the skill
Names what the skill's checks should measure and why, without building eval mechanics such as schemas, harnesses, or trigger queries
Surfaces unknowns as explicit open questions instead of silently assuming answers
evals.expected_output: A design brief: intent with trigger phrasings and near-miss negatives, a declared floor executor, commitlint config named as a source of truth to point at rather than copy, structure sketch, checks named by what they measure and why, open questions. No SKILL.md implementation.
evals.name: design-brief-commit-skill
evals.prompt: i keep hand-writing conventional commit messages for this repo and when teammates use claude half the scopes come out wrong. we already have commitlint set up (see commitlint config in the repo root). I want a skill the whole team can drop into claude code that gets commit messages right on the first try. before we build anything, help me design it properly — I don't want a SKILL.md yet, I want the design worked out first.

assertions: Reads the audited skill's files and cites evidence (quotes or file references) for its judgments
Asks the user questions to align on the skill's intent or usage, rather than delivering only one-way verdicts
Says plainly what it could not verify, rather than presenting every judgment with equal confidence
Converges on a short prioritized list of what matters most to fix, framed for the user to confirm
evals.expected_output: An audit conversation: evidence cited from the skill's actual files, questions to the user about intent and usage, unverifiable points named as such, and a prioritized set of proposed fixes framed for the user to confirm. No file modifications.
evals.name: audit-apache-age
evals.prompt: give skills/tech/apache-age an honest audit — is it actually well built? I want specifics cited from the files, not vibes. don't change any files, just tell me where it stands and what would matter most to fix.

assertions: Reads the full skill (SKILL.md and any references) before proposing changes
Each proposed change includes a reason and a predicted observable effect
Proposes a way to MEASURE triggering (e.g., labeled should/should-not-trigger test queries) rather than only asserting the new description is better
Proposes targeted edits, not a wholesale rewrite of the skill
Lists explicit questions for the user where needed information is missing
evals.expected_output: A proposed change set: full skill read first, each change with a reason and predicted observable effect, a proposed way to measure triggering, targeted edits rather than a rewrite, explicit questions where evidence is missing, no files modified.
evals.name: refactor-surface-intent
evals.prompt: the surface-intent skill (skills/thinkies/surface-intent) seems like it would fire on basically everything — its description says to use it at 'any moment you are adding to a system you have not fully read', which is nearly every write action. strengthen it so it triggers when it should, without gutting what makes it good. propose your changes before touching anything, and if you'd need information I haven't given you, list those questions.
